hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: It was a trip to celebrate our wedding anniversary, so when I mentioned that, we were welcomed with champagne and a chocolate cake in our room, along with decorations on the bed. There were many good points such as the spaciousness of the room, the overall size of the hotel grounds, the morning yoga class, the well-equipped spa and facilities, and the smiles and quick responses of the staff. Although the staff at other hotels were pleasant as well, the quality of the facilities might not be fully appreciated unless you stay there. If I had to mention one downside, it would be the limited number of restaurants. Since we have no intention of going out of the hotel, we might get bored after a few days... If that aspect were improved, I would like to stay longer.

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text:
What stands out is the extreme thoughtfulness of the staff. 

We traveled with a 6 month baby and all we asked for was a crib. But the staff went above and beyond and gave us a Diaper pail, a baby bath tub, baby toiletries, diapers, a sterilizer. The warmth and hospitality was unmatched. They made everything so smooth and easy for us. Will highly highly recommend anyone who comes to Ubud to stay at the Four Seasons. Staff goes out of their way to make you feel special and comfortable. They are always proactively helping guests with a smile on their face. 

Got purées on demand for baby. Tried Yoga and Pilates classes - they were great. Gym, jacuzzi and pool facilities were all top notch. Breakfast menu is great. Lovely views from the lobby & ayung terrace. Great cocktails at the bar. Lots of options for activities for both adults and kids. If there were more options for cuisines/restaurants for lunch/dinner, that would have added more variety when it comes to dining.

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: We used a pool villa for our honeymoon and it was great. It was my first time staying in such a room. I recommend the pool villa.

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: The hotel is located on a hillside by the Ayur River. The entire hotel is not very large, but the layout is complex, making it a bit difficult to find your way around when you first arrive. The scenery is beautiful, the service is excellent, and the dining is very delicious. In the evening, there is a special Indonesian dance performance, which is spectacular, especially the yoga class by the small river in the woods, providing a uniquely tranquil experience. Falling asleep at night to the sound of flowing water and insects is very pleasant. The only downside is that the overall facilities in the room are a bit outdated and have a somewhat vintage feel. Compared to the price, the cost-performance ratio is not very high.

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: I specifically chose Four Seasons Bali at Sayan as the venue of my marriage proposal to my girlfriend and I must say that the resort delivered beyond our expectations. Coincidentally, my girlfriend and I are also celebrating our 5th anniversary so I chose the most sought-after villa, the one-bedroom riverfront villa. Prior to our arrival, I was already communicating with the people of the resort since the proposal would be a surprise and it was seamless and hassle-free, which I already expected from a Four Seasons resort. I had several requests and concerns which they all addressed. Upon arrival, we did not undergo the usual check-in process and instead had a tour of the resort just to divert my girl's attention. Everyone did great in hiding everything from my girl. We had the most magical moment yet of our lives in lotus pond and had a romantic dinner as well after she said yes to me. For the villa, it was pure heaven. It has the softest bed ever! It's complete with modern facilities and very tastefully appointed. The private pool is just lovely and the view of Ayung River's breathtaking! For food, everything's perfect from our romantic dinner to our breakfast buffet. But what makes Four Seasons Bali at Sayan really a standout is the customer deluxe. Almost everyone knew my name when we were there, greeting me left and right. The buggy service is also fast and reliable. Everyone cooperated with our surprise plan and I'm extremely happy for that. Our stay was truly magical and pure bliss. It's easy to see why Julia Roberts opted to stay on this resort when she filmed Eat Pray Love. Perfect! 1. Staff 2. location and nature 3. Jaw-dropping villa

Grounds are well manicured and just so beautiful - hard to leave the hotel...  Staff very pleasant and attentive.  Cons:  turn down service is so early - both times attendants came as we were dressing for dinner (6 pm)!  Hotel dining, very expensive - eat elsewhere if at all possible.  

Highly recommended and would love to return...   The quiet, calm and seclusion; very friendly and accommodating staff

2) No information/letter was provided from Hotel about the renovation of a part of resort. I felt slight unconfortable by the noise of renovation.
All others are perfect, quite recommendable! Magnificant valley view from main building

review_text: 思い通りにならないことがまったくない、完璧なホスピタリティでした。恵まれたロケーションを最大に活かした最高の施設とアクテビティ。様々なリクエストにも柔軟に対応いただき本当に気持ち良く滞在させていただきました。必ずまた次回もこちらを選びたいと思います。 充実した施設、豊富なアクティビティ、全スタッフの気持ちよい対応

review_text:
今まで出張のたびにいろんなホテルを勉強の為に泊まってきましたが

ホテルだと思いました。
今後、バリに行かれる方に是非このホテルをお勧めしたいと思います。
ヴイラは広さも十分、もてあますほどの広さ、プライベートもしっかりと
守られ、清掃も行き届き、サービスも行き届いておりました。

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: 感觉很好，酒店很舒服，置身于热带雨林也是度假的好选择。 安静，空气好，住宿舒适。

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: 总体不错，环境和安全措施都很到位。但是淋雨房两人洗澡后有渗水到外面的现象并且较严重。酒店赠送水果较单一，应该更多样。 服务、环境、房间设施

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: 预定的是河畔别墅，但入住后发现水声很吵，换了座靠山顶的，房间装修老了点但很舒适，泳池孩子特别喜欢，尤其是夜晚点燃一只雪茄，赖在庭院中的沙发里，四周被虫鸣包围，非常自得。服务也很周到，但餐饮不好无早餐，酒店占地面积很大，散步时基本见不到客人，很悠闲。不爽的是来接机的酒店司机居然中途会改道带你去购物，无法让人理解！ 园林、户型和的设计很出色大堂

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: 酒店提供附近地区的pick up，到达酒店稍微早了点，check in后先到酒店内随便逛逛，结果很快服务生在游泳池边找到了我们（也不知他怎么发现的，酒店面积可不小），入住后发现房间奇大，两个房间两个大露台，全部面向山景。酒店环境打造很好，面山靠河，还自有水稻田。半露天高台餐厅环境优雅，伴有乐队，菜品也还可口。酒店服务方面几无问题，提供到购物观光区的免费交通（商务车）和预约出租。总之基本上算符合顶级五星级酒店之各项要素，唯SPA房间有时需要提前预约。当然所费银两不菲哦。 环境很好、设施很好、酒店房间也很好

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: 抵达乌布四季酒店门口落车后惊喜就开始了，空中栈道通向酒店大厅的屋顶，屋顶莲塘浮萍和莲花绽放着，下木楼梯抵达酒店服务台，开房的时间很久，大约等侯了45分钟，是因为网络预订的客人比较不容易查询到，或是酒店销售部把名字录错到前台。我定了7间房，有85平米的套房，123平米的套房和200平米的别墅，还有200平米河景别墅，当天由于酒店的套房全部没有开放，在整修，所以全部用的200平米的别墅接待我们，非常值得一提的是，河景别墅虽然贵，但在雨季的时侯不能住，因为河的流水声太吵了，根本会让您崩溃而整夜难眠。四季酒店服务不错，但是如果需要把早餐送到客房里用的话，需要加服务费。别的都没有什么问题。整个酒店像是在稻田里，公园中，没有房屋的身影，只有屋顶，有的是看不见屋顶的，有的看得见。这个酒店非常值得一住。 特别，绿化植被丰富，整个酒店都掩映在绿丛中，仿佛座落在世外桃园，放眼望去根本看不见房子，只能看见屋顶，而进入房间后则是柳暗花明又一村

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: 凡事一个缘字，此次入住便如是。临时决定住四季，当天下单，运气极好从复式套房升到一卧室别墅。酒店进门处极不起眼，狭长的巷子进去是个亭子，沿着亭子走进去，豁然开朗。飞碟似的主体设计，峡谷里葱郁的植物扑面而来，顶部做成荷花池，峡谷对面硕大茂盛的植物像是和你在打招呼，还有远处渺渺炊烟，仿佛进入侏罗纪公园，此刻人是如此渺小。海边别墅住过，山里别墅还是头一次，一进门就觉得蚊香，灭蚊器，蚊帐似乎多了点，过了二十分钟你就知道这些东西用处有多大。山里生态环境极好，蚊子不说了，稻田里的青蛙，屋外的蜗牛，壁虎。对某些东西敏感的同志要慎重。酒店提供的巴厘岛/Ubud地图很实用。一卧室的房间出奇的大，空荡荡的，更衣室也很大，可惜时间有限，只有晚上才享受了一把室外游泳池。河畔下沉式别墅ms更有特色些，可价格也更贵。SPA，餐饮没来得及享受，倒是蹭了几分钟传统表演，很是惊艳。ubud好酒店颇多，平价的Komaneka，小众的Amandari（安缦），COMO shambhala，还有没见着在哪的Chedi，Samaya。激烈的竞争下四季常常有好的价格和升级，这么看起来，还是有些性价比的。下次去可能会选一家住住，不过对于四季还是很推荐的。 地点，稻田，河畔，房间

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text:
What stands out is the extreme thoughtfulness of the staff. 

We traveled with a 6 month baby and all we asked for was a crib. But the staff went above and beyond and gave us a Diaper pail, a baby bath tub, baby toiletries, diapers, a sterilizer. The warmth and hospitality was unmatched. They made everything so smooth and easy for us. Will highly highly recommend anyone who comes to Ubud to stay at the Four Seasons. Staff goes out of their way to make you feel special and comfortable. They are always proactively helping guests with a smile on their face. 

Got purées on demand for baby. Tried Yoga and Pilates classes - they were great. Gym, jacuzzi and pool facilities were all top notch. Breakfast menu is great. Lovely views from the lobby & ayung terrace. Great cocktails at the bar. Lots of options for activities for both adults and kids. If there were more options for cuisines/restaurants for lunch/dinner, that would have added more variety when it comes to dining.

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: Podobał nam się serwis w tym hotelu. Obsługa hotelowa była na najwyższym poziomie - wszyscy byli bardzo mili i zawsze bardzo pomocni. Czystość też była na wysokim poziomie. I jeszcze przepiękny ogród w którym położone są wille - to naprawdę robi wrażenie. Mieliśmy mieć pokój z widokiem na rzekę i pola ryżowe. Niestety nie było widoku na pola ryżowe - były one zarośnięte wysoką trawą i wyglądały trochę jak dzika, zapuszczona łąka na polskiej wsi niż pola ryżowe, które hotel pokazuje na swoich folderach reklamowych. Dodatkowo naszą willę co wieczór odwiedzał nietoperz, który wisiał nad naszą kuchnią i dokonywał tam swojej codziennej toalety. Każdego ranka mieliśmy cały blat kuchenny, zlew, podłogę w ok. zlewu obsraną przez nietoperza. Niestety, ale czuć było taki specyficzny zapach z tego powodu również po posprzątaniu tego przez obsługę. To było słabe. Dodatkowo poprosiliśmy o willę, która będzie cicha. I taka by była, niestety, po drugiej stronie rzeki znajduję się huśtawki, na których przez całe dnie huśtali się ludzie nad urwiskiem, oczywiście przy tym bardzo głośno krzycząc. To powodowało, że nie było ciszy.... Willa znajdowała się w pięknym miejscu, ale widać było już po niej, że jest nadgryzione zębem czasu. W naszej ocenie willa kwalifikuje się do odświeżenia, remontu. Ostatnią rzeczą, która nas rozczarowała to była siłownia - bardzo mała i wyposażona w niewielką ilość sprzętu.

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: Qualité du service et de la restauration Mauvais rapport qualité prix car infrastructures vieillissantes

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: Die ganze Hotelanlage ist traumhaft schön. Besonders gut gefallen hat uns die absolute Privatsphäre in unserer großzügigen Poolvilla mit der herrlichen Aussicht. Wer nicht laufen möchte, kann sich jederzeit einen Buggy bestellen, um sich zum Spa oder zu einem der Restaurants bringen zu lassen. Das Essen war sensationell, der Service exzellent. Ich habe es noch nie in einem Hotel dieser Größe erlebt, dass uns ein Kellner sagte: „Bitteschön- unsere Karte. Wir kochen Ihnen aber auch alles andere, was Sie sich wünschen, sofern wir die Zutaten besorgen können.“ Da waren wir wirklich sprachlos - und haben das Angebot nicht angenommen, denn die Auswahl in der Karte war so groß und verlockend, dass wir gar keine Extrawünsche äußern mochten. Hervorragend sind auch die Cocktails! Zudem sollte man sich den Night Spa am Flussufer nicht entgehen lassen. Die Atmosphäre unter dem Sternenhimmel mit dem Rauschen des Flusses ist etwas ganz Besonderes. Und wir können die vom Hotel organisierte private Rafting Tour sehr empfehlen. Das hat so viel Spaß gemacht, und August war ein toller Guide. Dieses Hotel gehört zu den schönsten, in denen wir je gewesen sind, und wir würden uns sofort wieder dafür entscheiden, wenn wir wieder nach Bali kommen.

hotel_name: Four Seasons Resort Bali at Sayan
review_text: Le cadre est splendide avec un niveau de service et une gentillesse du personnel exceptionnel. Les niveaux tarifs sont prohibits pour Bali. Nous sommes au niveau de prix des palaces en France. La qualité des fruits peuvent etre améliorées au petit déjeuner

room_type: Sands Premier Double Queen City View
review_text: It was the best of the best experience during my trip to Singapore~ From the moment I touched the room key to when I checked out, it was such a wonderful experience ❤️ After returning from swimming, I received a small cake shaped like Marina Bay Sands since it was a special occasion, which made me even happier~ I noticed they are adding a new Sands Hotel (4 towers) next to Sands; I don't know when it will be completed, but I'm looking forward to my next visit. You can use the rooftop pool before check-in (3 PM), so you can leave your luggage and swim, and you'll receive an email notification when your room is ready. Only one person from the family needs to sacrifice to get the room key 😁 It would be great if they could improve the process so that you could receive the room key at the rooftop when the room is ready. I had to go down to the lobby in my swimsuit, so I had to prepare something to put on in advance, and it was a bit embarrassing because there were many people in the lobby. I hope they consider these aspects from the guest's perspective. Aside from that, the location, the view (it was a city view with the Fullerton Hotel and Merlion directly in front), I had a happy time without missing anything.
All was good Check in tooks to long and have to to counter twice.

room_type: Sands Premier Double Queen Gardens by the Bay View Room
review_text:
My mom celebrated my 65th on our 2-night stay in Marina Bay Sands.

The staff from the concierge (Ms. Patricia and Manager-on-Duty Ms. Judith) helped me set up my mom's birthday surprise decorations in the room. 

The restaurant staff in Spago (Ms. Felm, Mr. Stephen, Ms. Sheila and Ms. Riza) made our stay even more special doting and assisting on my senior mother while maintaining excellent service with the other guests. This is the world class service one would expect from Marina Bay Sands.

While it is undoubtedly an expensive hotel, the quality and experience fully justify the cost. The infinity pool is breathtaking, offering panoramic views of the Singapore skyline. Moreover, although this trip was special for us, it was the hotel that made those memories even more amazing. The facilities, service, and atmosphere all came together at a high level, creating an experience that made us genuinely feel, "I'm so glad I stayed here."

The rooms felt incredibly premium, and we especially appreciated the thoughtful details throughout the room. For example, they provided organizers for charging cables, and when they noticed glasses in the room, they even placed a special cleaning cloth for them. These small touches made the experience feel very personalized and luxurious.

The housekeeping staff were also exceptionally sweet and attentive. When they realized we were traveling with our daughter, they prepared lovely surprises for her, including chocolates, children’s toiletries, a toothbrush, and even towel toys. These thoughtful gestures made our stay feel extra special and memorable.

The pool area was beautiful as well, and overall the atmosphere of the hotel was amazing. We genuinely enjoyed our stay very much. The check-in process took quite a while and could perhaps be more efficient. However, we really appreciated that the team allowed us early access to our room, which made a very positive difference after our arrival.

One important thing to mention is that our room was located on the third floor, and unfortunately we could hear a lot of noise coming from the central area of the hotel, even late at night. We could still hear guests returning from parties around 3 AM, and despite the presence of security, the noise situation did not really improve. It was quite disturbing at times. For this reason, we would definitely recommend requesting a higher-floor room when booking, as we personally regretted not asking for one.

Overall, despite this issue, we had a fantastic experience and would happily stay here again.

Hotel was amazing and the room service staff was incredible Checking in - my room was meant to be ready by 3pm especially as I checked in early morning. When I arrived back to pick up my keys at 5pm,  I was told my room was still not ready and I needed to be ready for a 6pm dinner. I expressed extreme dissatisfaction and the reason given was because the earlier guest had late check-out which is not my problem. For a luxury hotel I was extremely disappointed by this.

The location and views of Singapore were amazing. For first time visitors to Singapore we couldn’t have chosen a better place to enjoy our short stay. Finally, yes the pool was amazing too and it was well worth waking up early to see the sunrise and have a swim! It was a bit frustrating to have to wait in line to get breakfast at the roof restaurant but once in the breakfast was great.

It was the best hotel we have ever stayed at & the pillows & beds were the most comfortable that we have ever experienced. It was such a nice end to our holiday & to celebrate my birthday there. The staff even brought me a birthday cake up to our room, which was a special touch.
We had a city room and a garden room. Both had fabulous views!!! The rooms were both beautiful. We loved it all! It was a little noisy due to the construction of the fourth tower.

We only stayed two nights to experience some of Singapore’s sights.  This place is absolutely immaculate. We had a premier king room with city view and it was just a dream.  The facilities and furnishings are top notch. Swimming pool is lovely, but can get slightly noisy, so don’t expect it to be somewhere to lie in peace and quiet.  Special thanks to all the lifeguards, they are the silent hero’s sitting there watching and protecting all the guests enjoying themselves.  When we were there a massive thunderstorm hit and they immediately got everyone safely out of the pool.  Also a big thanks to Danny at the Luggage Storage area who looked after our bags and got our Grab drivers attention to park close to the door and he loaded the bags all safely in the car. The staff have all been trained impeccably to have such beautiful manners.  Was a delight to stay here. If I was going to be picky, the only thing I could say was I felt the main entry was too large and airy for the elegance of the rest of the building . Felt more like an auditorium not an elegant and lush interior like the rest of the building.

Breakfast is available either as a buffet at the Ground Floor or from the menu at the rooftop restaurant.
Personally, I prefer the rooftop restaurant, as the service and food quality are better.

As the hotel was recently renovated, the room design is thoughtfully done.
In particular, the indoor lighting and air-conditioning control panel are exceptionally well designed — the best I’ve experienced among the hotels I’ve stayed at.
Overall, the room feels extremely comfortable. The seating at the buffet area is rather cramped, and the soundproofing in the rooms could be improved.

Wonderful room and amenities. Great views from the room balcony, the pool and the skypark. Very efficient staff who were more than happy to attend to all your needs very promptly. Is in an ideal location for travellers that like to gamble, shop and go on long walks. The hotel lobby looks more like a shopping mall than a hotel. There are no chairs, sofas, tables in the lobby as in common in hotels. The lobby is more like a thoroughfare for people going to the casino, shopping mall or the gardens by the Bay. There is a constant stream of people going to the oberservation deck, both hotel guests and regular people paying a fee to visit the deck. There is no priority for hotel guests. The pool gets closed during storms/rains so plan your visit to the pool as soon as you can. Be ready to wait for lifts going to these areas as not all lifts in all towers go there. This hotel is not for regular people who want a personal experience as such an experience is catered for high rollers. The Piaza, which is highly regarded, offers this, but is super expensive. While this is understandable considering the number of people who stay there and the people visiting the casino, I still think guests staying in regular rooms should experience just a sliver of this personal experience considering what they are paying.

Arrived tired after a long trip from Kyoto for the last 2 nights of our honeymoon. Front of house staff were aware of this and congratulated us as we were helped to our room and shown around. It's this level of attention that's the norm there.

Room - large, stylish, massive comfy bed, nespresso coffee (we had missed our coffee so much!), big bathroom with large tub and separate shower, dressing room and all this with an incredible view over Tokyo. 
Pool/gym - complimentary unlike so many others in Japan. 2 gym rooms with amazing views out over the city. Pool warm enough for kids to bob about in but not too warm if you train properly. Split into 2 lanes for lane swimming and 2 lanes for whatever. Never busy.

Bar - on the top floor. Amazing views at night. Extensive cocktails working out at around £11 each. No cover charge and live jazz every night. Great end to an evening. 

Restaurants - we only ate at the Japanese restaurant. Again view amazing. Food was presented immaculately, very tasty. Portions slightly larger then we were used to from traditions Japanese places but this is probably as they cater for plenty of westerners. Slightly more expensive then eating out but we were happy with the price. Probably the third best meal we ate in 3 weeks and we are food people (went to Japan for the food and weren't disappointed).

Concierge - had dealings with multiple incredibly polite staff who all spoke excellent English. They helped us booking restaurants and the recommendations turned out to be excellent. Very knowledgable about Tokyo. 

Overall - best place we've ever stayed. Went the extra mile to make our honeymoon perfect. We will be back  Stylish big rooms with amazing views, the extra effort the staff go to make it a perfect stay, the pool and gym on the 47th floor (and not having to pay extra like so many Japanese hotels)

The Park Hyatt is destination luxury hotel in Tokyo and is priced accordingly. The rooms are comfortable and spacious. The dining options are elegant and refined with a breakfast that is not to be missed. Front door, restaurant, bar, cafe, housekeeping and room services staff are efficient and friendly and the service provided by them was exceptional. The senior staff at the hotel in reception and concierge seemed distracted most of the time and most questions for service or booking help that we asked were not actioned or not met. Most responses from senior staff offered only excuses not any alternatives.

So better checking on the room service orders would be in place. Also rather difficult to find the entrance!

From the moment we stepped through the doors, the classy staff at Park Hyatt Tokyo made us feel welcome and assisted us all the way to our room, showcasing exceptional hospitality. The hotel's stylish artistic decor, even inside the elevators, caught our eyes and added to the luxurious ambiance. The lobby area was a sight to behold with beautiful trees adorning the top of the skyscraper, creating a serene escape amidst the city buzz. Nestled in the heart of Shinjuku, the location couldn’t have been better, allowing us easy access to explore around. The room was spacious and offered awe-inspiring views of Mount Fuji which was a sight we cherished. The iconic pool at the top of the building came with a stunning panorama of the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building and Shinjuku station, providing a unique and refreshing experience. The famous New York Bar lived up to its reputation and gave us our 'Lost in Translation' moment, with a classy setting and a buzzing atmosphere. Starting our day with an elegant breakfast at Girandole was the cherry on top. Our stay was filled with luxury, comfort, and unique experiences, making Park Hyatt Tokyo a destination we'd love to revisit.

L'ultimo giorno passato in Giappone è stato motivo di un regalo personale e pertanto abbiamo optato per una suite che affacciava al 43º piano dell'hotel mitologico per me ovvero l'hotel dove è stato registrato il film "Lost in translation". Sulle tracce del film siamo andati alla ricerca degli ambienti dove è stato girato ma sfortunatamente proprio il bar New York e il ristorante grill New York erano chiusi ed in rifacimento. Peraltro siamo venuti a conoscenza che l'albergo verrà chiuso a breve  per un lungo tempo proprio perché verrà demolito per essere ristrutturato nuovamente. La suite, cosa si potrebbe dire di una stanza di quasi 100 m² che affaccia al 43º piano su tutta Tokio veramente poco. La qualità degli ambienti, la pulizia, le accortezze, i servizi proposti sono da ovviamente un hotel di alto livello e le aspettative non sono state tradite. Abbiamo cenato in uno dei ristoranti presenti all'interno dell'albergo in stile giapponese, Kozue, optando per due tipologie di degustazioni. Sono rimasto veramente stupito dalla quantità eccessiva di cibo proposto ed il numero delle portate che erano infinite e che ovviamente non sono riuscito a terminare proprio per il numero delle stesse. Al 47º piano si può utilizzare una piscina meravigliosa affacciata su Tokio oppure per gli amanti del fitness utilizzare la palestra che venne usata in una delle scene più esilaranti da Bill Murray. Sicuramente l'hotel appare dentro un po' datato e la ristrutturazione imminente gioverà a quest'aspetto più appesantito. Difficile migliorare una struttura così attenta all'ospite

値段通りの環境とサービス 事前に息子が来ると話していたけど、すぐに案内してもらえなかった事。ニューヨークグリルで日本人の親子客は私達位だったけど受付で名前を言っても予約していたにも関わらずすぐにわからなかった事。窓側の席の希望はかなったが出来ればスカイツリーが見える窓側の方がよかった。あと、以前はケ−キに花火がついて、生演奏の人がハッピーバースデイを歌っていたけど今回はなかった。息子の二十歳の誕生日記念で利用したのでもう一つ上のサービスを期待した。ただ、思った以上に客が多く無理だったかもしれないので、次回宿泊の時に期待します。ほ
